body: As part of the **Fiat-Shamir specification** grant, this issue tracks the development of a formal specification of the Fiat-Shamir transformation based on duplex sponges in ArkLib. Currently absent from the `ArkLib/ProofSystem` hierarchy.

comments.body: Current-repo audit correction: the duplex-sponge Fiat-Shamir development is not absent; it lives under `ArkLib/OracleReduction/FiatShamir/DuplexSponge/**`, not `ArkLib/ProofSystem/**`. Focused checks pass for the basic canonical closeout and DSFS soundness file, but the strict residual census still reports DSFS key-lemma/foundation residuals. More detailed closeout tracker: #314.

First residual discharged (`4d92ec755`): **`Lemma512Honest.lean`** — `Lemma5_12HonestResidual` (CO25 Lemma 5.12, honest form: `¬E(tr) → ¬E_inv_honest(tr, s, S)`), via a `HasInvEntry` trace analysis with no probability content. 0 sorry, axiom-clean.
Claiming this now. I pulled the latest #316/#314 discussion; I’ll start from the current  residual map, keep  synced, and post progress as individual DSFS bricks land.

Clean claim note: I am working this thread now. Scope is ArkLib/OracleReduction/FiatShamir/DuplexSponge/** from the #314 residual map; I will keep main synced and comment as individual DSFS residual bricks land.

Progress pushed to main: `1c157fe2a` (`fix(#316): repair DSFS inverse bad-event M2a proof`). This repaired the earlier `Lemma512Honest.lean` build gap by aligning `capacitySegmentDupPermInv` with the inverse answer-capacity branch used by the M2a argument, fixing the `getElem?` proof, and correcting the final witness order. Verified: `lake build ArkLib.OracleReduction.FiatShamir.DuplexSponge.Security.BadEvents` and `lake build ArkLib.OracleReduction.FiatShamir.DuplexSponge.Security.Lemma512Honest` both pass; `lemma5_12_honest` prints only `[propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound]`. Residual census after the push still shows the next DSFS M2 targets as `Lemma5_14HonestResidual` and `Lemma5_16HonestResidual`, plus the birthday/hybrid-step residuals.

Second #316 brick pushed: `862a72808` (`feat(#316): expose DSFS no-inverse trace-index brick`). This adds `DuplexSpongeFS.Sponge316.not_inv_getElem?_of_not_E`, the direct `getElem?` form of the M2a no-inverse consequence needed by the `J_BT` first-occurrence payloads for the M2b/M2c fork and timing arguments. Verified with `lake build ArkLib.OracleReduction.FiatShamir.DuplexSponge.Security.Lemma512Honest`; the new theorem prints only `[propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound]`.

Third #316 brick is on main: \ (in the autosync batch; current main has since advanced to \). The DSFS-relevant change exposes the first-occurrence payloads from \: \ returns the recorded hash query, and \ returns the recorded forward-or-inverse permutation query for any nonterminal chain step. This is the trace-index bridge needed before using \ in the M2b/M2c fork/timing arguments. Verified: \ and \.

Correction to the previous malformed shell-expanded note: third #316 brick is on main at 84ec551de (autosync batch; current main has since advanced to 0b9452f08). The DSFS-relevant change exposes BacktrackSequence.Index first-occurrence payloads: index_hash_getElem? returns the recorded hash query, and index_perm_getElem?_of_lt returns the recorded forward-or-inverse permutation query for any nonterminal chain step. This is the trace-index bridge needed before using forward_getElem?_of_not_E_of_perm_or_inv in the M2b/M2c fork/timing arguments. Verified: lake build ArkLib.OracleReduction.FiatShamir.DuplexSponge.Security.Backtrack and lake build ArkLib.OracleReduction.FiatShamir.DuplexSponge.Security.Lemma512Honest.

Fourth #316 brick pushed to main at 79e965145. DSFS-relevant change: Lemma512Honest.lean now exposes jbt_hash_getElem? and jbt_perm_forward_getElem?_of_not_E. The first packages the J_BT hash first-occurrence payload as a concrete trace getElem? fact; the second combines the Backtrack permutation first-occurrence payload with the no-inverse consequence off E, so any nonterminal J_BT permutation index is the forward p query. Verified with lake build ArkLib.OracleReduction.FiatShamir.DuplexSponge.Security.Lemma512Honest; both new theorems print only propext, Classical.choice, and Quot.sound.

Fifth #316 brick pushed to main at 35ea821a2. DSFS-relevant change: Lemma512Honest.lean now exposes jbt_time_h_outputState_nonempty and jbt_time_h_first_perm_forward_getElem?_of_not_E. These package the E_time_h_honest witness shape: a hash-after-first-permutation J_BT payload cannot be the empty chain, and off E its first chain index is the forward p query for step 0. Verified with lake build ArkLib.OracleReduction.FiatShamir.DuplexSponge.Security.Lemma512Honest; both new theorems print only [propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound].

Sixth #316 brick pushed to main at d10201467. DSFS-relevant change: Lemma512Honest.lean now exposes e_time_h_honest_raw_forward_witness_of_not_E. This turns an off-E E_time_h_honest witness into concrete raw trace facts: the J_BT hash entry, the first chain forward permutation entry, step-zero index equality, and the strict raw-trace ordering needed by the eventual dedup collision proof. Verified with lake build ArkLib.OracleReduction.FiatShamir.DuplexSponge.Security.Lemma512Honest; the new theorem prints only [propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound].

Taking the next #316 brick now: concrete hash-entry preservation through `removeRedundantEntryDS`. I read the latest thread through the sixth brick at `d10201467`; this will stay below the M2c probability/collision proof and just provide the dedup bridge needed to transport the raw `E_time_h_honest` hash anchor into the deduplicated trace. I will validate `Lemma512Honest.lean`, push this as a separate main commit, and report the exact theorem/axiom status.

Seventh #316 brick pushed to main at 7435b2d82. DSFS-relevant change: Lemma512Honest.lean now exposes HasHashEntry plus hasHashEntry_removeRedundant_of_mem, proving that concrete raw hash anchors survive removeRedundantEntryDS. This is the dedup-side bridge needed to move the E_time_h_honest hash anchor from the raw witness into the base trace used by BadEventDS.E. Verified with lake build ArkLib.OracleReduction.FiatShamir.DuplexSponge.Security.Lemma512Honest; hasHashEntry_removeRedundant_of_mem prints only [propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound].

Taking the next small #316 brick: promote the checked `J_BT` hash-anchor first-occurrence fact into a nonredundancy theorem. This is still below the full M2c collision proof, but it is the exact local fact needed to argue the timing witness hash anchor is not removed by the DSFS dedup pass.

Eighth #316 brick is on main at 72c8ff515 in the pushed stack now ending at 6bf276320. DSFS-relevant change: Backtrack.lean now exposes BacktrackSequence.index_hash_no_prior, and Lemma512Honest.lean packages it as jbt_hash_no_prior. This records that the J_BT hash index is the first occurrence of its hash anchor, so no earlier raw trace slot contains the same hash entry. This is the first-occurrence guard needed with hasHashEntry_removeRedundant_of_mem when transporting the E_time_h_honest hash anchor through dedup. Verified with lake build ArkLib.OracleReduction.FiatShamir.DuplexSponge.Security.Backtrack ArkLib.OracleReduction.FiatShamir.DuplexSponge.Security.Lemma512Honest; jbt_hash_no_prior prints only [propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound].

Ninth #316 brick is on main at 9b03dfc71 in the pushed stack now ending at f19067641. DSFS-relevant change: Backtrack.lean now exposes BacktrackSequence.index_perm_no_prior_of_lt, and Lemma512Honest.lean packages it as jbt_perm_no_prior_of_lt. This is the permutation-side first-occurrence guard: for any nonterminal J_BT chain index, no earlier raw trace slot contains either the matching forward entry or the corresponding inverse entry. It complements jbt_hash_no_prior for the M2c dedup and ordering bridge. Verified with lake build ArkLib.OracleReduction.FiatShamir.DuplexSponge.Security.Backtrack ArkLib.OracleReduction.FiatShamir.DuplexSponge.Security.Lemma512Honest; jbt_perm_no_prior_of_lt prints only [propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound].

Tenth #316 brick pushed to main at b314ba36e. DSFS-relevant change: Lemma512Honest.lean now exposes not_redundantEntryDS_hash_of_no_prior and jbt_hash_not_redundant. This upgrades the J_BT hash first-occurrence guard into the exact one-step dedup fact: the hash anchor index itself cannot satisfy redundantEntryDS. That is the local nonremoval brick needed before proving the full E_time_h_honest raw-to-dedup collision step. Verified with lake build ArkLib.OracleReduction.FiatShamir.DuplexSponge.Security.Lemma512Honest; jbt_hash_not_redundant prints only [propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound].

Eleventh #316 brick pushed to main at d86fa9522. DSFS-relevant change: Lemma512Honest.lean now exposes e_time_h_honest_raw_forward_capacity_witness_of_not_E. This packages the off-E E_time_h_honest witness as the raw collision shape before dedup: a concrete J_BT hash entry, a strictly earlier first forward permutation entry, and the equality that the forward input capacity is the anchoring hash capacity. Verified with lake build ArkLib.OracleReduction.FiatShamir.DuplexSponge.Security.Lemma512Honest; the new theorem prints only [propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound].

Twelfth #316 brick pushed to main at a37e747d4. DSFS-relevant change: Lemma512Honest.lean now exposes E_of_base_hash_after_forward_capacity. This packages the target BadEventDS.E constructor: if the deduplicated trace contains a hash entry and a strictly earlier forward permutation entry sharing that hash capacity on either input or output side, capacitySegmentDupHash fires. This is the base-trace endpoint for the M2c raw-to-dedup bridge. Verified with lake build ArkLib.OracleReduction.FiatShamir.DuplexSponge.Security.Lemma512Honest; E_of_base_hash_after_forward_capacity prints only [propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound].

Thirteenth #316 brick pushed to main at e6b695c9d. DSFS-relevant change: Lemma512Honest.lean now names the shared predicate HasForwardCapacityBeforeHash, proves E_of_base_hasForwardCapacityBeforeHash for deduplicated base traces, and proves e_time_h_honest_raw_hasForwardCapacityBeforeHash_of_not_E for raw traces from the off-E timing witness. This separates the remaining hard step into a single preservation problem: move HasForwardCapacityBeforeHash through removeRedundantEntryDS. Verified with lake build ArkLib.OracleReduction.FiatShamir.DuplexSponge.Security.Lemma512Honest; both new public theorems print only [propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound].

Fourteenth #316 brick pushed to main at aa31edeb6. DSFS-relevant change: Lemma512Honest.lean now exposes HasFirstHashForwardCapacityBeforeHash, hasForwardCapacityBeforeHash_of_first, and e_time_h_honest_raw_hasFirstHashForwardCapacityBeforeHash_of_not_E. This strengthens the raw M2c collision-shape witness with the first-occurrence guard on the hash anchor, so the remaining dedup preservation step can keep a nonredundant hash target while chasing the earlier forward-capacity witness through erased redundant entries. Verified with lake build ArkLib.OracleReduction.FiatShamir.DuplexSponge.Security.Lemma512Honest; the new theorem prints only [propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound].

Fifteenth #316 brick pushed to main at 0f477577b. DSFS-relevant change: Lemma512Honest.lean now exposes redundant_forward_capacity_prior. This is the forward-witness replacement fact needed for the dedup preservation step: when a redundant forward permutation entry is erased, its redundancy certificate gives an earlier forward entry with the same or reversed state pair, and the capacity equality is preserved on the corresponding side. Verified after rebasing onto f891bcc1f with lake build ArkLib.OracleReduction.FiatShamir.DuplexSponge.Security.Lemma512Honest; the new theorem prints only [propext, Quot.sound].

Sixteenth #316 brick pushed to main at 01aefbc34. DSFS-relevant change: Lemma512Honest.lean now has private get_eraseIdx_before and get_eraseIdx_after helpers. These package the raw List.eraseIdx index-shift facts as get-value equalities for entries before and after the erased slot, removing one source of proof noise for the upcoming one-step preservation of HasFirstHashForwardCapacityBeforeHash. Verified after rebasing onto ed9797865 with lake build ArkLib.OracleReduction.FiatShamir.DuplexSponge.Security.Lemma512Honest. No new public theorem was added in this brick.

Seventeenth #316 brick pushed to main at f55abdadc. DSFS-relevant change: Lemma512Honest.lean now exposes hasForwardCapacityBeforeHash_removeRedundant_of_first, e_time_h_honest_dedup_hasForwardCapacityBeforeHash_of_not_E, and not_e_time_h_honest_of_not_E. This discharges the hash-timing half of M2c by transporting the strengthened first-hash forward-capacity witness through removeRedundantEntryDS and then firing E_of_base_hasForwardCapacityBeforeHash against BadEventDS.E. The remaining honest timing work is the separate E_time_p_honest half. Verified after rebasing onto 01aefbc34 with lake build ArkLib.OracleReduction.FiatShamir.DuplexSponge.Security.Lemma512Honest; the new public theorem prints only [propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound].

Eighteenth #316 brick pushed to main at 2415658b9 (autosync). DSFS-relevant change: Lemma512Honest.lean now exposes hasFirstHashForwardCapacityBeforeHash_hash_not_redundant on top of the f55abdadc hash-timing dedup proof. This packages the strengthened first-hash witness into the exact anchor fact: a concrete hash entry together with proof that the hash slot itself cannot satisfy redundantEntryDS. Verified after rebasing onto f55abdadc with lake build ArkLib.OracleReduction.FiatShamir.DuplexSponge.Security.Lemma512Honest; the theorem prints only [propext, Quot.sound].

Nineteenth #316 brick pushed to main at 3be4f5f8c. DSFS-relevant change: Lemma512Honest.lean now exposes jbt_time_p_next_outputState_bound, e_time_p_honest_raw_adjacent_forward_witness_of_not_E, and e_time_p_honest_raw_forward_capacity_witness_of_not_E. This packages the remaining E_time_p_honest side up to the raw adjacent-forward shape: the successor chain permutation query is a real nonterminal forward entry, it appears earlier in the raw trace than its predecessor, and the predecessor output capacity equals the successor input capacity. This is the permutation-side analogue of the raw M2c payload before the remaining dedup/base-event transport step. Verified after rebasing onto 51c8fc4c8 with lake build ArkLib.OracleReduction.FiatShamir.DuplexSponge.Security.Lemma512Honest; the new theorem prints only [propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound].

Twentieth #316 brick pushed to main at 41cd9da41. DSFS-relevant change: Lemma512Honest.lean now exposes HasInputCapacityBeforeForwardOutput and E_of_base_hasInputCapacityBeforeForwardOutput. This names the base-trace endpoint for the E_time_p_honest path: once dedup produces a later forward permutation whose output capacity matches an earlier forward permutation input capacity, BadEventDS.E fires through capacitySegmentDupPerm. This complements the nineteenth raw adjacent-forward witness; remaining work is the raw-to-dedup transport for this predicate. Verified after rebasing onto d3716d899 with lake build ArkLib.OracleReduction.FiatShamir.DuplexSponge.Security.Lemma512Honest; the new theorem prints only [propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound].

Twenty-first #316 brick pushed to main at 13f09f4df. DSFS-relevant change: Lemma512Honest.lean now exposes HasForwardCapacityBeforeForwardOutput, hasForwardCapacityBeforeForwardOutput_of_input, and E_of_base_hasForwardCapacityBeforeForwardOutput. This broadens the permutation-timing base endpoint to match either side of an earlier forward permutation entry, which is necessary because erasing a redundant earlier forward slot can replace it with the reversed forward pair and move the preserved capacity equality from input side to output side. Verified after rebasing onto 5daf73e6f with lake build ArkLib.OracleReduction.FiatShamir.DuplexSponge.Security.Lemma512Honest; the new theorem prints only [propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound]. Remaining #316 work is now the strengthened raw current-forward first-occurrence predicate plus removeRedundantEntryDS transport into this broader endpoint.

Twenty-second #316 brick pushed to main at `4641cfec7`. DSFS-relevant change: `Lemma512Honest.lean` now exposes `e_time_p_honest_raw_hasInputCapacityBeforeForwardOutput_of_not_E` and `e_time_p_honest_raw_hasForwardCapacityBeforeForwardOutput_of_not_E`. This packages the raw `E_time_p_honest` adjacent-forward witness as both the original input-side predicate and the broader preservation-friendly predicate introduced in the previous brick. Verified after rebasing onto `1d39d2c7d` with `lake build ArkLib.OracleReduction.FiatShamir.DuplexSponge.Security.Lemma512Honest`; both new theorems print only `[propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound]`. Remaining work is the current-forward first-occurrence/nonremoval predicate and the `removeRedundantEntryDS` transport into `HasForwardCapacityBeforeForwardOutput`.

Twenty-third #316 brick pushed to main at 630278150. DSFS-relevant change: Lemma512Honest.lean now exposes HasFirstForwardCapacityBeforeForwardOutput, hasFirstForwardCapacityBeforeForwardOutput_current_not_redundant, and hasForwardCapacityBeforeForwardOutput_removeRedundant_of_first. This proves the generic dedup transport for the permutation-timing side once the later forward anchor is protected against the same-direction reversal shape used by redundantEntryDS: erasing a redundant earlier witness is repaired via redundant_forward_capacity_prior, and erasing the protected current anchor is impossible. Verified after rebasing onto c4c764e7f with lake build ArkLib.OracleReduction.FiatShamir.DuplexSponge.Security.Lemma512Honest; the new public bridge prints only [propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound], and the current-anchor nonredundancy theorem prints only [propext, Quot.sound]. Remaining work is the raw-to-strong-current guard: the existing J_BT no-prior lemma rules out the forward/inverse first-occurrence pair, while this transport predicate needs no prior same-or-reversed forward entry because that is the shape redundantEntryDS actually erases.

Twenty-fourth #316 brick pushed to main at 0e0149157. DSFS-relevant change: Lemma512Honest.lean now exposes HasPriorReversedForwardAnchor, e_time_p_honest_raw_hasFirstForwardCapacityBeforeForwardOutput_or_prior_reverse_of_not_E, and not_e_time_p_honest_of_not_E_of_no_prior_reverse. This isolates the remaining permutation-timing gap exactly: off E, an E_time_p_honest witness either already gives the strengthened first-forward predicate that transports through removeRedundantEntryDS, or there is a strictly earlier same-direction reversed forward entry for the J_BT anchor. The conditional closure proves the E_time_p_honest half once that obstruction is excluded. Verified after rebasing onto e986a16b0 with lake build ArkLib.OracleReduction.FiatShamir.DuplexSponge.Security.Lemma512Honest; the two new public theorems print only [propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound]. Remaining work is to rule out or route the HasPriorReversedForwardAnchor obstruction under the in-tree redundantEntryDS semantics.

Twenty-fifth #316 brick pushed to main at ad38b6007. DSFS-relevant change: Lemma512Honest.lean now exposes HasRedundantForwardAnchor, hasRedundantForwardAnchor_of_prior_reverse, prior_reverse_of_hasRedundantForwardAnchor_of_not_E, hasPriorReversedForwardAnchor_iff_hasRedundantForwardAnchor_of_not_E, and not_e_time_p_honest_of_not_E_of_no_redundant_forward_anchor. This characterizes the remaining M2c permutation-timing obstruction as exactly a redundant nonterminal J_BT forward anchor under the in-tree redundantEntryDS semantics: off E, the prior same-direction reversed-forward obstruction and redundant-anchor predicate are equivalent, and the conditional E_time_p_honest closure can now be stated directly as a no-redundant-anchor invariant. Verified after rebasing onto ac1dd8872 with lake build ArkLib.OracleReduction.FiatShamir.DuplexSponge.Security.Lemma512Honest; the new public theorems print only [propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound]. Remaining work is to prove that no-redundant-anchor invariant from the intended BackTrack/dedup semantics, or to route the now-isolated redundant-anchor case into the base bad-event definitions.

Twenty-sixth #316 brick pushed to main at f6d40176a. DSFS-relevant change: Lemma512Honest.lean now exposes lemma5_16_honest_of_no_prior_reverse, a conditional full M2c assembly theorem at the exact Lemma5_16HonestResidual interface. It combines the already-discharged hash-timing half with the conditional permutation-timing half, so a global exclusion of HasPriorReversedForwardAnchor is sufficient to close the honest timing residual. Verified after rebasing onto ad38b6007 with lake build ArkLib.OracleReduction.FiatShamir.DuplexSponge.Security.Lemma512Honest; the new assembly theorem prints only [propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound]. With the adjacent ad38b6007 brick, the same remaining obstruction is also characterized as a no-redundant-forward-anchor invariant under the current redundantEntryDS semantics.

Twenty-seventh #316 brick pushed to main at 367a96650. DSFS-relevant change: Lemma512Honest.lean now exposes lemma5_16_honest_of_no_redundant_forward_anchor, the full Lemma5_16HonestResidual assembly theorem phrased at the no-redundant-anchor boundary introduced in ad38b6007. It combines not_e_time_h_honest_of_not_E with not_e_time_p_honest_of_not_E_of_no_redundant_forward_anchor, so the remaining target is now the single global invariant that nonterminal J_BT forward anchors are not redundant under the current redundantEntryDS semantics. Verified with lake build ArkLib.OracleReduction.FiatShamir.DuplexSponge.Security.Lemma512Honest; the new theorem prints only [propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound].

Twenty-eighth #316 brick pushed to main at 078d0a2b5. DSFS-relevant change: Lemma512Honest.lean now exposes no_redundant_forward_anchor_of_noRedundantEntryDS, not_e_time_p_honest_of_not_E_of_noRedundantEntryDS, and lemma5_16_honest_of_noRedundantEntryDS. This records the exact deduped-trace route: if the trace supplied to the honest timing residual already satisfies NoRedundantEntryDS, then the redundant J_BT forward-anchor obstruction is impossible and the full Lemma5_16HonestResidual assembly follows. Verified with lake build ArkLib.OracleReduction.FiatShamir.DuplexSponge.Security.Lemma512Honest; the new public theorems print only [propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound]. The remaining raw-trace gap is therefore the routing/statement question: either run the timing residual over the deduped base trace, or repair redundantEntryDS so the current raw-trace semantics match the CO25 opposite-direction redundancy shape.

Twenty-ninth #316 brick pushed to main at bd1a45a79. DSFS-relevant change: Lemma512Honest.lean now exposes not_e_time_honest_of_not_E_of_noRedundantEntryDS and rewires lemma5_16_honest_of_noRedundantEntryDS through it. This packages the fixed-trace deduped full timing closure: the hash half uses not_e_time_h_honest_of_not_E, and the permutation half uses not_e_time_p_honest_of_not_E_of_noRedundantEntryDS. Verified from a clean worktree after rebasing onto b8d03b4ea with lake build ArkLib.OracleReduction.FiatShamir.DuplexSponge.Security.Lemma512Honest; the new theorem and deduped assembly print only [propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound]. Scratch note: I also tested the opposite-direction redundantEntryDS repair in /tmp/arklib-issue316-redundancy-1781094146. The focused build fails in Lemma512Honest at the forward/inverse redundancy inversions and the current M2a hasInvEntry preservation route, so that semantic repair needs a broader inverse-aware dedup transport/M2a route rather than a one-token safe change.

Thirtieth #316 brick pushed to main at 7633da7a7. DSFS-relevant change: Lemma512Honest.lean now exposes not_e_time_honest_removeRedundantEntryDS_of_not_E, instantiating the fixed-trace NoRedundantEntryDS closure on the canonical base trace returned by removeRedundantEntryDS. This is the explicit endpoint for rerouting M2c over the deduped trace: any BackTrack family over (removeRedundantEntryDS tr).1 has no honest timing event off E of that base trace. Verified after rebasing over the latest #232 main commits with lake build ArkLib.OracleReduction.FiatShamir.DuplexSponge.Security.Lemma512Honest; the new theorem prints only [propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound].

Thirty-first #316 brick pushed to main at f6adeca53. DSFS-relevant change: BadEvents.lean now proves removeRedundantEntryDS is a fixpoint on NoRedundantEntryDS traces and that the combined bad event is invariant under deduping its own base trace (BadEventDS.E_removeRedundantEntryDS_iff). Lemma512Honest.lean uses this to expose not_e_time_honest_removeRedundantEntryDS_of_not_E_raw, the deduped-base timing closure from the original raw hypothesis ¬ E tr. This makes the deduped M2c route usable from downstream raw-trace assumptions without pretending the false raw Lemma5_16HonestResidual is dischargeable. Verified after rebase with lake build ArkLib.OracleReduction.FiatShamir.DuplexSponge.Security.BadEvents ArkLib.OracleReduction.FiatShamir.DuplexSponge.Security.Lemma512Honest; the new timing wrapper prints only [propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound].

Thirty-second #316 brick pushed to main at c62f9cf55. DSFS-relevant change: BirthdayBound.lean now imports the proven Sponge316 timing closure and exposes probEvent_dedupTimeHonest_le_probEvent_E, bounding honest timing events over (removeRedundantEntryDS tr).1 by the original raw trace event E tr. It also adds dedupTimeHonest_birthday_of_residual, so the eager Lemma5_8 birthday residual directly bounds deduped timing witnesses without consuming the refuted raw Lemma5_16HonestResidual. Verified after rebase with lake build ArkLib.OracleReduction.FiatShamir.DuplexSponge.Security.BirthdayBound; both new BirthdayBound theorems print only [propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound].

Thirty-third #316 brick pushed to main at 18911a140. DSFS-relevant change: BirthdayBound.lean now exposes probEvent_honestBadDedupTime_le_probEvent_E and honestBadDedupTime_birthday_of_residuals. These keep the raw M2a/M2b residual interface for inverse/fork events, but route timing through the deduped trace using the Sponge316 closure, so the assembly no longer needs the refuted raw Lemma5_16HonestResidual for the timing side. Verified after rebase with lake build ArkLib.OracleReduction.FiatShamir.DuplexSponge.Security.BirthdayBound; both new mixed-route theorems print only [propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound].

## Ladder-lane status relevant to the #316 timing reroute (2026-06-10)

The deduped-timing reroute (bricks 26–33 above) is now superseded-in-direction by ground truth established on #314 today:

1. **Exhaustive probe** (`scripts/probes/probe_co25_def55_paper_semantics.py`, landed at `5abc7d1c2`): CO25 Def 5.5 **paper semantics** (opposite-direction certificates) has **zero** counterexamples to ¬E→¬E_time and ¬E→¬E_fork over 51.7M small traces; the in-tree same-direction semantics has 1,956 (including a minimal 3-entry one: `[h, (p,a,b), (p,b,a)]`). The `Lemma516TimePFalse` countermodel dies under paper semantics. So the right long-term disposition is the wave-4 `BadEvents.lean` surgery (claimed on #314), after which raw Lemma 5.16 (and 5.14) become provable and the dedup-reroute wrappers can be retired or kept as transport conveniences.
2. **Salt-threading landed** (`1f0861a0c`): the §5.8 ladder compiles end-to-end with the memoized salted `Hyb₃` and the salted hit-only `Hyb3Strict`.
3. **New refutations** (see #314 for detail): the unsalted `Hyb34StepResidual` is false at every δ (verifier-log shape: memoized bridges are query-silent on hits while `V.fiatShamir` logs per round) on top of the δ≥1 salt-grinding; `Lemma5_8EagerBirthdayResidual` is false as stated (E_pinv self-firing clause). The in-flight repair set: wave-4 BadEvents surgery (other lane) + salted/log-canonicalized eager surface `Hyb0S–Hyb4S` + `KeyLemmaEagerSaltedResidual` assembly (this lane, in flight).
4. **F0a landed** (`5abc7d1c2`): `KeyLemma.ηStar` now carries the paper exponent (Eq. 5), so the apex `KeyLemmaResidual` is no longer numerically over-strong.

Net for #316: the duplex-sponge FS track's open math is now fully enumerated, three false statements carry (or will carry) machine-checked refutation records instead of silently blocking, and every remaining open leg has either a probe-backed repair direction or a brick-level proof plan.

**Post-#314 frontier transfer (2026-06-10).** #314 closed with the grant-scope targets met; the remaining DSFS key-lemma math now tracks here. Live frontier (all over the repaired `DuplexSpongeFS.Paper.EPaper` surface; plans + architecture notes in the #314 thread):

1. **`Lemma5_8EagerPaperResidual`** (`BirthdayBoundPaper.lean`) — the single live probability core. 3-step plan: eager-PRP/lazy-RF carrier coupling (Lean-ecosystem-first; the accumulator + collision/landing bricks in `BirthdayBound.lean` are ready), `EPaper` decomposition into capacity collision/landing families, budget recombination (`BudgetCover`). Consumed by `honestBad_birthday_of_paperResidual` (already proven).
2. **`Hyb01StepResidual`** — 5.8 applied at the Hyb₀/Hyb₁ trace length + the §5.6 off-event coupling.
3. **`Hyb12StepResidual`/`Hyb23StepResidual`** — memoized-ladder couplings: per-entry line-4 pushforward (Δ_{ψ⁻¹}=0 exact) resp. φ-image reachability invariant + per-fresh-key uniform-preimage identity (salted keying makes 5.23's exact-0 plausible as stated).
4. **`Hyb34StepResidual` at δ=0** — εB = strict-replay collapse via `memoCoherentTable_keystone` (salt subsingleton at δ=0); εA = verifier-segment birthday (same engine as 1).
5. **Consolidation**: `BadEventDSPaper` (`BadEventsPaper.lean`) is half-repaired (B2 only — machine-checked: any inverse entry still fires it at j'=j). Either apply the one-line B1 fix + prove the two events equal, or retire it onto `EPaper`; transport `Lemma512HonestPaper`'s direction-agnostic chain-recording bricks either way.

Audit update (2026-06-11): the comment-stripped shortcut scan found no live `axiom`/`constant`/`opaque`, no `sorry`/`admit`, and no `: True` stubs. In the duplex-sponge Fiat-Shamir area, most `def ... : Prop` hits are ordinary bad-event predicates, but the explicit residual obligations are:

- `ArkLib/OracleReduction/FiatShamir/DuplexSponge/Security/BirthdayBoundPaper.lean:91` `Lemma5_8EagerPaperResidual`

## Complementary hypothesis slate for the Lemma-5.8 probability core (the one live math item)

The frontier item 1 is, mathematically, the classic **PRP/PRF switching lemma** (Bellare–Rogaway 2006 code-based game-playing / Impagliazzo–Rudich birthday form) specialized to the duplex-sponge capacity slice. Constraint/prior-art audit + hypotheses, complementary to the in-flight 3-step plan:

**K1 (provenance, larp-guard):** no Lean formalization of the switching lemma exists (Mathlib has none; FCF/EasyCrypt proofs are Coq/Why3-side, not portable) — so the eager-PRP/lazy-RF coupling being built here (bricks 4A/4B-2/4B-3) is the FIRST in this ecosystem; worth stating the generic lemma at the `uniformOfFinset`-permutation level (not the sponge level) so it lands in ToMathlib/VCVio as the reusable artifact. The 4B-3 pushforward (`map_onestep_uniform`) is already the heart; hypothesis: the generic statement `Pr[distinguish PRP from RF in q queries] ≤ q(q−1)/2^{c+1}` follows from iterating 4B-3 + the landed collision/landing accumulators with NO sponge-specific input.

**K2 (the δ=0 shortcut):** frontier item 4's salt-subsingleton observation generalizes: hypothesis — at δ=0 the ENTIRE Hyb ladder (items 2–4) degenerates to two applications of K1's generic lemma + the proven `memoCoherentTable_keystone`, bypassing the per-entry pushforward analyses of item 3; check before building the general-δ machinery whether the grant scope needs δ>0 at all.

**K3 (B2-half consolidation):** for item 5, the machine-checked B2-only repair suggests the honest move is retiring `BadEventDSPaper` onto `EPaper` (one event surface, not two equal ones) — the 'prove the two events equal' branch creates a permanent equivalence-maintenance burden for zero consumers.

**A1 (coupling functor):** items 2–4 are all 'adjacent hybrids differ by one oracle-table edit, coupled by an identity-off-one-entry bijection' — hypothesis: one `TableEditCoupling` lemma (parameterized by the edit and the off-event) subsumes the three step residuals; the diff of the three posted plans is exactly the edit parameter. **A2 (birthday tightness):** the matching attack (collision forcing at q ≈ 2^{c/2}) is cheap to state against the same carrier and would make the bound provably tight — no formal system has a TIGHT switching lemma; modest extra work over K1's accumulators.
